heading: Grants to facilitate the siting of interstate electricity transmission lines
label: 18715a Grants to facilitate the siting of interstate electricity transmission lines
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of , and not as part of div. D of , which enacted this chapter.Pub. L. 117–169Pub. L. 117–58
DefinitionsFor definitions of “Secretary” and “State” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 50111 of Pub. L. 117–169section 17113b of this title

children.heading: Appropriation
children.label: a Appropriation
children.text: In addition to amounts otherwise available, there is appropriated to the Secretary for fiscal year 2022, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $760,000,000, to remain available through , for making grants in accordance with this section and for administrative expenses associated with carrying out this section.September 30, 2029

children.label: A
children.text: Studies and analyses of the impacts of the covered transmission project.

children.label: B
children.text: Examination of up to 3 alternate siting corridors within which the covered transmission project feasibly could be sited.

children.label: C
children.text: Participation by the siting authority in regulatory proceedings or negotiations in another jurisdiction, or under the auspices of a Transmission Organization (as defined in ) that is also considering the siting or permitting of the covered transmission project.section 796 of title 16

children.label: D
children.text: Participation by the siting authority in regulatory proceedings at the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission or a State regulatory commission for determining applicable rates and cost allocation for the covered transmission project.

children.label: E
children.text: Other measures and actions that may improve the chances of, and shorten the time required for, approval by the siting authority of the application relating to the siting or permitting of the covered transmission project, as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Economic development
children.label: 2 Economic development
children.text: The Secretary may make a grant under this section to a siting authority, or other State, local, or Tribal governmental entity, for economic development activities for communities that may be affected by the construction and operation of a covered transmission project, provided that the Secretary shall not enter into any grant agreement pursuant to this section that could result in any outlays after .September 30, 2031

children.heading: Final decision on application
children.label: 1 Final decision on application
children.text: In order to receive a grant for an activity described in subsection (b)(1), the Secretary shall require a siting authority to agree, in writing, to reach a final decision on the application relating to the siting or permitting of the applicable covered transmission project not later than 2 years after the date on which such grant is provided, unless the Secretary authorizes an extension for good cause.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: 2 Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of an activity described in subparagraph (C) or (D) of subsection (b)(1) shall not exceed 50 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: to a siting authority upon approval by the siting authority of the applicable covered transmission project; and

children.label: B
children.text: to any other State, local, or Tribal governmental entity upon commencement of construction of the applicable covered transmission project in the area under the jurisdiction of the entity.

children.heading: Returning funds
children.label: d Returning funds
children.text: If a siting authority that receives a grant for an activity described in subsection (b)(1) fails to use all grant funds within 2 years of receipt, the siting authority shall return to the Secretary any such unused funds.

children.label: i
children.text: at a minimum of 275 kilovolts of either alternating-current or direct-current electric energy by an entity; or

children.label: ii
children.text: offshore and at a minimum of 200 kilovolts of either alternating-current or direct-current electric energy by an entity; and

children.label: B
children.text: for which such entity has applied, or informed a siting authority of such entity’s intent to apply, for regulatory approval.

children.heading: Siting authority
children.label: 2 Siting authority
children.text: The term “siting authority” means a State, local, or Tribal governmental entity with authority to make a final determination regarding the siting, permitting, or regulatory status of a covered transmission project that is proposed to be located in an area under the jurisdiction of the entity.
